name: Oxybutynin
risk: Cognitive decline, delirium, falls, constipation, urinary retention. Opposes donepezil.

name: Diphenhydramine
risk: Sedation, delirium, falls, anticholinergic toxicity

name: Amitriptyline
risk: Strong anticholinergic, cardiac conduction, orthostatic hypotension, sedation

name: Chlorpromazine
risk: High anticholinergic, sedation, orthostatic, EPS

name: Paroxetine
risk: Most anticholinergic SSRI. Hyponatremia, drug interactions (CYP2D6)

name: Olanzapine
risk: STRONGEST anticholinergic among common antipsychotics. Weight gain, metabolic syndrome

name: Diazepam
risk: Long-acting. Falls, sedation, cognitive impairment, paradoxical agitation

name: Zolpidem
risk: Falls, confusion, complex sleep behaviors, fractures

name: Temazepam
risk: Falls, sedation, next-day impairment

name: Glyburide
risk: Prolonged hypoglycemia (long half-life, active metabolites). Use glipizide instead

name: Megestrol
risk: Thrombotic events, minimal appetite benefit, fluid retention, adrenal suppression

name: Doxazosin
risk: Orthostatic hypotension → falls. Not first-line for HTN

name: Metoclopramide
risk: EPS, tardive dyskinesia (risk increases with duration). Max 12 weeks

name: Sliding-scale insulin
risk: Reactive dosing without proactive management. Hypoglycemia risk

name: Meperidine
risk: Neurotoxic metabolite (normeperidine) → seizures. NEVER use in elderly

name: Donepezil
risk: GI side effects (take with food, NOT on empty stomach). Does NOT prevent postop delirium

name: Haloperidol
risk: EPS, QTc prolongation. Lower anticholinergic than olanzapine

name: Quetiapine
risk: Sedation, orthostatic, metabolic. Moderate anticholinergic

name: Risperidone
risk: EPS at higher doses, orthostatic. Low anticholinergic

name: Metformin
risk: CI if CrCl<30. Lactic acidosis rare. Causes anorexia/weight loss in thin elderly. Hold 48h pre-op

name: Alendronate
risk: Esophagitis (take upright with water). CI in reflux esophagitis → use denosumab

name: Apixaban
risk: Safest DOAC in CKD. CI with mechanical valves. Dose reduce if ≥2: age≥80, weight≤60kg, Cr≥1.5

name: Sertraline
risk: Hyponatremia risk (like all SSRIs). Generally safe in elderly. Low anticholinergic

name: Bupropion
risk: LOWEST hyponatremia risk among antidepressants. Seizure risk at high doses. Activating

name: Mirtazapine
risk: Sedation (good if insomnia), weight gain (good if anorexic). Monitor metabolic

name: Nitrofurantoin
risk: Avoid if CrCl<30: pulmonary toxicity, peripheral neuropathy, hepatotoxicity

name: Nifedipine IR
risk: Reflex tachycardia, hypotension, risk of CV events. Use long-acting formulations instead

name: Cyclobenzaprine
risk: Anticholinergic, sedation, falls, ineffective for spasticity in elderly. Avoid >2 weeks

name: Methocarbamol
risk: Sedation, dizziness, falls. Limited efficacy. Avoid in elderly

name: Promethazine
risk: Strong anticholinergic, sedation, EPS, falls, delirium. Avoid in elderly

name: Clonidine
risk: CNS depression, rebound hypertension, bradycardia. High risk of adverse CNS effects in elderly. Avoid as first-line antihypertensive.

name: Digoxin
risk: Narrow therapeutic index. Avoid doses >0.125mg/day in elderly. Toxicity risk with renal impairment, hypokalemia. Monitor levels.

name: Ketorolac
risk: High GI bleeding risk, AKI. Avoid in elderly especially >5 days. Beers: avoid all non-COX-selective NSAIDs chronically.

name: Hydroxyzine
risk: Strong anticholinergic. Sedation, confusion, urinary retention, dry mouth. ACB=3. Use alternatives for anxiety/pruritus.

name: Chlorpropamide
risk: Prolonged hypoglycemia (long half-life 36h). SIADH/hyponatremia. Beers: avoid in all older adults. Use short-acting agents.

name: Prazosin
risk: Orthostatic hypotension, syncope, falls. Beers: avoid as antihypertensive. High first-dose syncope risk.

name: Amiodarone
risk: Thyroid toxicity, pulmonary fibrosis, hepatotoxicity, QT prolongation. Beers: avoid as first-line for AF. Multiple drug interactions.

name: Carisoprodol
risk: Sedation, dependence potential, anticholinergic effects. Metabolized to meprobamate (anxiolytic). Beers: avoid.

name: Trimethobenzamide
risk: Extrapyramidal effects, limited efficacy. Beers: avoid. One of least effective antiemetics.

name: Ibuprofen
risk: GI bleeding (3-4x risk in elderly), AKI, HTN, HF exacerbation. Avoid with anticoagulants, CKD, HF. Max 5 days if used.

name: Ferrous Sulfate >325mg
risk: Constipation, nausea. Doses >325mg/day do not increase absorption but increase side effects. Beers: avoid high doses.

name: Nitrazepam
risk: Long-acting benzo. Falls, fractures, cognitive impairment, delirium. Beers: avoid all benzos in elderly.

name: Chlordiazepoxide
risk: Long half-life (24-48h). Accumulation in elderly, prolonged sedation, falls. Beers: avoid.

name: Trihexyphenidyl
risk: Strong anticholinergic for Parkinson's. Confusion, hallucinations, urinary retention, constipation. ACB=3. Avoid in elderly.

name: Benztropine
risk: Anticholinergic for EPS. Cognitive impairment, urinary retention, constipation. ACB=3. Beers: avoid. Use lower risk alternatives.

name: Desipramine
risk: Less anticholinergic than amitriptyline but still risky: orthostatic hypotension, cardiac conduction delays. Beers: avoid.

name: Nortriptyline
risk: Secondary amine TCA — less anticholinergic but still: orthostatic hypotension, QT prolongation. Beers: avoid. SNRI preferred.

name: Insulin (sliding scale only)
risk: Sliding scale alone = poor glycemic control + hypoglycemia risk. Beers: avoid without basal insulin. A1c targets should be relaxed in frail elderly.

name: Propoxyphene
risk: Withdrawn in many countries. Cardiac toxicity, seizures, minimal analgesic benefit. Beers: avoid. Use paracetamol or low-dose opioids instead.

name: Pentazocine
risk: Mixed agonist-antagonist. CNS effects including confusion, hallucinations. Beers: avoid.

name: Ticlopidine
risk: Safer alternatives exist (clopidogrel). Neutropenia, TTP risk. Beers: avoid.

name: Tolterodine
risk: Anticholinergic for OAB. Dry mouth, constipation, confusion in elderly. ACB=3. Consider mirabegron as alternative.

name: Solifenacin
risk: Antimuscarinic for OAB. Cognitive effects, constipation, dry mouth. ACB=3. Beers: avoid if possible.
